channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: How China and U.S. eased the Middle East oil shock and kept prices from spiking even higher
item.description: China and the U.S., the world's two largest economies, wield great influence over the oil market and are using it to help plug the supply gap.

item.title: Stephen Miran exits the Fed. How he set the stage for Kevin Warsh.
item.description: The outgoing Fed governor shares big ideas with the incoming chair.

item.title: Trump told Xi ‘I don't talk about’ whether U.S. would defend Taiwan from China
item.description: President Donald Trump spoke about Taiwan after a two-day visit to China, where he said he discussed Iran, and trade deals with Chinese President Xi Jingping.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall, strained by tech pullback and yields spike: Live updates
item.description: Wall Street is coming off yet another positive session, with both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closing at record highs.

item.title: 30-year Treasury yield tops 5.1%, highest in nearly a year
item.description: Treasurys spiked on Friday as inflation signals continue to muddy interest rate expectations under the new Federal Reserve chair Kevin Warsh.

item.title: The stock market rally can keep going despite Friday's pullback. How to stay long while hedging slightly
item.description: Jeff Kilburg breaks down this put spread options strategy.

item.title: Inflation rate projected to hit 6% in the second quarter, top economic forecasters say
item.description: The recent surge in inflation is likely to get worse over the next several months, according to a survey Friday.

item.title: Bristol Myers Squibb turns to China to develop new drugs in newest cross-continent collaboration
item.description: U.S. drugmaker Bristol Myers Squibb inked a partnership with a Chinese company that some say could mark the next wave of collaboration across continents.

item.title: Starbucks to lay off 300 U.S. employees, shutter some regional support offices
item.description: Starbucks said the moves will help the coffee chain return to profitable growth.

item.title: Trump-Xi summit: The 3 big takeaways from historic meeting in Beijing
item.description: The two-day meeting wrapped up Friday, setting the tone for further U.S.-China talks this year.

item.title: Maine might boot Sen. Susan Collins. It could hurt the state's wallet for years
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins is the lone New England Republican left in Congress and chairs the Senate Appropriations Committee.

item.title: Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry lunch auction fetches $9 million for charity
item.description: Buffett, Curry and his wife Ayesha Curry will join the winner and their guests on June 24 in Omaha, Nebraska, where Berkshire Hathaway is based.

item.title: Detroit automakers have cut more than 20,000 U.S. salaried jobs as AI threat looms
item.description: Reasons for the job declines vary by automaker, but are generally tied to evolving technological changes in the industry — including the rise of AI.

item.title: China will buy more U.S. oil because it is a natural trade partner, says Energy Secretary Wright
item.description: China relies heavily on crude oil imports from the Middle East but those supplies are mostly cut off due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Seth Klarman’s Baupost makes Amazon top holding, starts Aon and Visa stakes
item.description: The hedge fund boosted its stake in Amazon by 47% in the first quarter, making the e-commerce giant its largest equity holding at the end of March.

item.title: Cerebras stock falls 10% after blockbuster IPO debut — here's what's happening
item.description: Cerebras Systems' shares were lower in early trading Friday, after its blockbuster stock market debut on Thursday.

item.title: Bill Ackman built Microsoft stake in first quarter during sell-off, betting on AI and cloud growth
item.description: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management has built a position in Microsoft, the billionaire hedge fund manager said Friday in a post on X.

item.title: UAE fast tracks second West-East oil pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The second pipeline project comes as global energy supplies remain under pressure and flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain severely limited

item.title: Magnum Ice Cream stock soars after report of potential private equity takeover
item.description: It comes just six months after Magnum spun off from Unilever to create the world's largest standalone ice cream maker.

item.title: Too far, too fast? Stocks like Micron are very stretched compared to historical trading patterns
item.description: CNBC Pro screened the S&P 500 for stocks trading the furthest above or below their 200-day moving averages, relative to their own historical trading patterns.

item.title: Amazon takes further aim at the grocery market with expanded 30-minute delivery
item.description: Amazon is bringing its 30-minute delivery service to more U.S. cities. That's a good thing for investors.

item.title: How to plan a trip for medical tourism and the best resources
item.description: If you're seeking a medical procedure abroad, these resources can help.

item.title: Why Britain’s potential next PM is putting investors on edge
item.description: Traders fear a new left-leaning U.K. government led by Andy Burnham would challenge the country's fiscal discipline.

item.title: Nvidia's trillion-dollar run puts pressure on the bulls
item.description: Nvidia's market cap is now just shy of $5.7 trillion, up from $4.7 trillion as of market close last Tuesday.

item.title: 'Survivor’s penalty’ can follow the death of a spouse — but its impact may be smaller than expected
item.description: The ‘survivor’s penalty’ can hit retirees after a spouse dies. But the impact could be smaller than expected, experts say.

item.title: CIA chief makes historic trip to Cuba as US blockade chokes island's energy supplies
item.description: The U.S. has been restricting oil shipments to Cuba since January, plunging the country into blackouts lasting days at a time.

item.title: Family investors turn to old-economy businesses like dealerships and fisheries to avoid AI disruption
item.description: While car dealerships and fisheries are less eye-catching than high-flying AI startups, they offer stability and cash flow that's attractive to family offices.

item.title: SpaceX IPO prospectus could land as soon as next week, sources say
item.description: Elon Musk's reusable rocket company is getting closer to what's expected to be a record stock market debut.

item.title: Oil prices jump after Trump says he is losing patience with Iran
item.description: Oil prices rose Friday on news that China has agreed to purchase oil from America.

item.title: Bond market believes Fed behind the curve on inflation as Warsh takes over
item.description: Bond traders are hoping that the central bank's easing bias is replaced with a skewed view toward tightening.
